title: chionanthus mala-elengi
intro: Chionanthus mala-elengi, also known as the White Chrysanthemum or East Indian Medinilla, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Chionanthus mala-elengi include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Chionanthus mala-elengi is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and powders, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional Indian medicines, such as Ayurvedic formulations.
